name: Cattle Hill Wind Farm
notable: 44x Vestas V126-3.45MW. Cattle Hill Wind Farm Holdings (Goldwind subsidiary). Launched Feb 2020.

timeline.title: EPBC Referral 2009/4707
timeline.detail: Cattle Hill Wind Farm — Referral Decision

timeline.title: Aurora Energy PPA
timeline.detail: Long-term agreement with Aurora Energy for RECs

timeline.title: Construction commenced
timeline.detail: Goldwind/PowerChina began construction at Lake Echo

timeline.title: Commercial operations commenced
timeline.detail: Commencement date from AEMO registration via OpenElectricity.

timeline.title: First generation recorded
timeline.detail: First market dispatch data recorded in AEMO systems.

eis_specs.document_title: Cattle Hill Wind Energy Facility Environmental Impact Assessment (TAS EPA)
eis_specs.hub_height_note: Standard 97 m hub height for V126-3.45 MW on Tasmanian central plateau
eis_specs.wind_speed_period: Mean annual wind speed at hub height; Tasmanian Central Highlands — from 3-year on-site met mast, one of Australia's highest wind resource sites
eis_specs.energy_yield_method: WAsP modelling with Tasmanian mesoscale wind atlas calibration; P50 net yield
eis_specs.connection_substation_name: Repulse 110 kV substation (TasNetworks), Central Highlands TAS
eis_specs.connection_distance_note: ~14 km of new 110 kV line to TasNetworks Repulse substation on the Tasmanian 110 kV backbone network.
eis_specs.connection_augmentation: New 110 kV connection to TasNetworks. Line protection and fault level management at Repulse substation upgraded. TasNetworks Marrawah Wind Farm to Cattle Hill system strength assessment conducted.
eis_specs.notes: 144 MW (42 × 3.45 MW Vestas V126), Central Highlands Tasmania. Developer: Tilt Renewables (sold to Wild Cattle Hill Pty Ltd / Pacific Hydro / PowAR). TAS EPA assessment approved 2017; EPBC approval also obtained. Now operating. Tasmanian Central Highlands is among Australia's highest onshore wind resource (>9 m/s mean). 44% P50 capacity factor is exceptional by Australian standards. TasNetworks 110 kV connection — TAS uses 110 kV backbone (vs 132/220/330 kV on mainland).
